channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Common Elements That Make a Yard Look Tacky
item.description: The article discusses four common elements that can make a front yard appear tacky, focusing on aesthetic and maintenance issues. First, neglect such as overgrown shrubs, dirty walkways, and peeling p...

item.title: Springboks Prepare for Crucial Nations Championship Match Against Wales
item.description: The Springboks and Welsh coaching teams enjoyed a South African braai at Umhlanga Rock, but the friendly atmosphere ends as they prepare to face each other in a Nations Championship match at Kings Par...

item.title: Government invests 5.76bn to enhance Lindi transport infrastructure
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced a major infrastructure project aimed at improving transportation in the Lindi region through the acquisition of a ferry. The initiative, valued at 5.76 billion T...

item.title: Australia's AI Strategy Needs Expertise and Governance to Address Key Threats
item.description: Prime Minister Albanese's speech on Australia's AI strategy highlights the need for centralized governance, expertise, and sovereignty in addressing critical threats. The article argues that fragmente...

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson seeks Trump's support for budget measures amid GOP strategy shifts
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-LA) is actively seeking President Donald Trump's endorsement for his proposals, which include funding the government ahead of the September 30 deadline and pushing for a ...

item.title: One Nation Leader's Podcast Interview with Tommy Robinson Examined for Party Impact
item.description: The article explores the implications of Pauline Hanson's recent podcast interview with far-right British activist Tommy Robinson, questioning whether this collaboration could cause internal divisions...

item.title: Pensioner reunites with R2,000 found at ATM after two weeks of social media search
item.description: An elderly man named Ron Sabor accidentally left R2,000 at an ATM in Elsies River, prompting his family to initiate a search for the owner. The family, who discovered the cash, took proactive steps by...

item.title: Philippines Diplomatically Protests China's Alleged 'Moral Bankruptcy' in South China Sea Dispute
item.description: The Philippine Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has formally protested a Chinese state media video depicting the country as having 'moral bankruptcy,' which critics argue is an attempt to undermine...

item.title: Israel Proposes Crocodile-Ringed Prisons Inspired by Trump's Alligator Alcatraz Plan
item.description: The Israeli government has reclassified Nile crocodiles as 'specially managed wild animals' to advance a proposal inspired by the defunct Trump administration's Alligator Alcatraz immigration detentio...

item.title: Hanson Attributes Immigration Challenges to Historical Policy Shifts
item.description: Pauline Hanson, leader of the One Nation party, discussed immigration issues in a podcast with UK far-right activist Tommy Robinson. She claimed Australia's immigration problems began in 1973 when the...

item.title: Ramaphosa Urges Namibia and South Africa to Focus on Beneficiation and Regional Value Chains
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has called on Namibia and South Africa to move beyond exporting raw materials, emphasizing the need to stop 'exporting opportunities while importing prosperity'...

item.title: Ombudsman confirms ongoing investigation into former Budget Secretary's flood control issues
item.description: The Ombudsman, Jesus Crispin Remulla, clarified that former Budget Secretary Amenah Pangandaman remains part of the investigation into the flood control controversies despite her reappointment to gove...

item.title: Calgary Stampeders and Montreal Alouettes Rivalry Intensifies in CFL Match
item.description: The Calgary Stampeders and Montreal Alouettes faced off in a highly anticipated CFL rematch, with the Alouettes securing a 37-30 victory. The game highlighted the intense rivalry between the Philpot b...

item.title: Understanding Hasad-Envy: Its Impact and Spiritual Dangers in the Ummah
item.description: The article discusses the harmful effects of hasad-envy (jealousy) in Islamic teachings, highlighting its destructive nature on the heart and soul. It outlines three levels of envy: mild dissatisfacti...

item.title: Babulal Marandi demands judicial inquiry into JSDMS scam allegations
item.description: Leader of Opposition Babulal Marandi has written to Chief Minister Hemant Soren demanding a judicial inquiry into alleged financial scams in the Jharkhand Skill Development Mission Society (JSDMS). Th...

item.title: Chris Hayes Warns of Close Election Margins in Texas Amid Midterm Concerns
item.description: Chris Hayes, host of 'All In,' discusses his concerns about the 2026 midterm elections and the potential for close races in key states like Texas. He highlights the risks posed by President Donald Tru...

item.title: Andy Burnham's leadership transition: Challenges and preparedness
item.description: The article explores Andy Burnham's ascension as Labour leader, focusing on his team's efforts to prepare for a potential government. It highlights the three-phase plan for his first two weeks in offi...

item.title: Supreme Court mandates medical treatment for sexually assaulted children
item.description: The Supreme Court has clarified that doctors cannot refuse treatment to children who have been sexually assaulted, emphasizing the legal obligation to provide medical care. The ruling underscores the ...

item.title: DeChambeau faces penalty over golf rules controversy at British Open
item.description: American golf star Bryson DeChambeau has been penalised two strokes at the British Open after officials ruled his actions on the fifth hole violated golf regulations. The incident occurred when DeCham...

item.title: Clacton By-Election Sees Record 34 Candidates Amid Political Controversy
item.description: A total of 34 candidates have been confirmed for the Clacton constituency by-election, triggered by the resignation of Nigel Farage. The Reform UK leader resigned as MP on 7 July following increased s...
